title: UnblockableNonCollidersOpen
tex_block_html: Note that unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open, regardless of the subset $C \ins V \cup J$.

% Claim statement file for row claim_3_21 -- "UnblockableNonCollidersOpen".
% Auto-generated stub by scaffold/solve_chapter.py at row start. The
% manager agent (or a worker it dispatches) fills the body below with the
% claim's statement(s) from the lecture notes -- statement only, no proof

%
% This file is a sibling of `main.tex` inside `<subsection>/tex/`. The
% `subfiles` package lets it render both standalone and as part of
% `main.tex`. Note: `main.tex` does NOT \subfile this statement file -- the
% sibling `_proof_` file restates the statement above the proof, so
% including both in `main.tex` would be redundant. This file exists for
% standalone reading / grep convenience.

\documentclass[main]{subfiles}

\begin{document}
% ref: claim_3_21 (statement)
% title: UnblockableNonCollidersOpen
\def\rowref{claim\_3\_21}
\phantomsection\label{claim_3_21}

Note that unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open, regardless of the subset $C \ins V \cup J$.

\end{document}
tex_statement.html: Note that unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open, regardless of the subset $C \ins V \cup J$.

addition_to_the_LN: [claim_is_tautology_against_def_3_17] This statement is a direct unfolding of Definition 3.17, whose $\sigma$-open and $\sigma$-blocking criteria range only over colliders and blockable non-colliders. Unblockable non-colliders are therefore unconstrained for every $C \subseteq V \cup J$, and the row carries no substantive proof obligation beyond this definitional observation.
